stage1: CONTENT: A message-flow diagram with THREE dashed muted-teal rounded Node containers in a row (dashed borders only, no black outer box): "Node 1" (left), "Node 2" (middle), "Node 3" (right). Node 1 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler" and a steel-blue "Worker 1"; inside Worker 1 a small white "Ownership Table" with columns [Obj | Task | Loc] and two rows: X and Y. Node 2 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker 2", and a muted-teal "Object Store". Node 3 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker 3", and a muted-teal "Object Store". Small numbered cool-grey circle badges (1,2,3...) sit on the arrows to show step order. THIS STAGE — Scheduling task B: show ink-black arrows for these steps: (1) Worker 1 -> its own Scheduler asking to run B; (2) Node 1 Scheduler forwards to Node 2 Scheduler; (3) Worker 1 writes into its Ownership Table that B will run on Node 2 (row X, Loc = N2); (4,5) Node 2 Scheduler assigns Worker 2; (6) Worker 1 submits B to Worker 2. Worker 2 is highlighted steel-blue (about to run B). Node 3 is drawn faded/pale blue-grey (inactive this stage). One ochre-red caption: "the submitter (Worker 1) keeps the books".
stage2: CONTENT: A message-flow diagram with THREE dashed muted-teal rounded Node containers in a row (dashed borders only, no black outer box): "Node 1" (left), "Node 2" (middle), "Node 3" (right). Node 1 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler" and a steel-blue "Worker 1"; inside Worker 1 a small white "Ownership Table" with columns [Obj | Task | Loc] and two rows: X and Y. Node 2 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker 2", and a muted-teal "Object Store". Node 3 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker 3", and a muted-teal "Object Store". Small numbered cool-grey circle badges (1,2,3...) sit on the arrows to show step order. THIS STAGE — Executing B, producing big object X: Worker 2 runs B and creates a big object, shown as a steel-blue box "Object X" placed INSIDE Node 2's muted-teal Object Store, with a small ochre-red PIN icon. A dotted arrow from Node 2 updates a bottom "Object Table" (light box, columns [Obj | Locs]) with row "X | N2". An ink-black arrow from Worker 2 back to Worker 1 ("B done"); Worker 1's Ownership Table marks X available. Node 3 faded pale blue-grey. One ochre-red caption: "big object X stays in place, only its location is registered".
stage3: CONTENT: A message-flow diagram with THREE dashed muted-teal rounded Node containers in a row (dashed borders only, no black outer box): "Node 1" (left), "Node 2" (middle), "Node 3" (right). Node 1 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler" and a steel-blue "Worker 1"; inside Worker 1 a small white "Ownership Table" with columns [Obj | Task | Loc] and two rows: X and Y. Node 2 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker 2", and a muted-teal "Object Store". Node 3 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker 3", and a muted-teal "Object Store". Small numbered cool-grey circle badges (1,2,3...) sit on the arrows to show step order. THIS STAGE — Scheduling C and copying X: ink-black arrows: Worker 1 -> Node 3 Scheduler to run C; Node 3 Scheduler finds dependency X missing and queries the bottom "Object Table"; the Object Table replies "X is on N2"; then a bold ink-black arrow copies "Object X" from Node 2 Object Store to Node 3 Object Store. The X copy in Node 2 has an ochre-red PIN icon (pinned); the X copy in Node 3 is drawn in pale blue-grey with a small "LRU" tag (cached, not pinned). One ochre-red caption: "home copy is pinned, remote cache can be evicted".
stage4: CONTENT: A message-flow diagram with THREE dashed muted-teal rounded Node containers in a row (dashed borders only, no black outer box): "Node 1" (left), "Node 2" (middle), "Node 3" (right). Node 1 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler" and a steel-blue "Worker 1"; inside Worker 1 a small white "Ownership Table" with columns [Obj | Task | Loc] and two rows: X and Y. Node 2 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker 2", and a muted-teal "Object Store". Node 3 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker 3", and a muted-teal "Object Store". Small numbered cool-grey circle badges (1,2,3...) sit on the arrows to show step order. THIS STAGE — Executing C, returning small object Y: Worker 3 is highlighted steel-blue, reads "Object X" from Node 3's muted-teal Object Store, runs C(X). Worker 3 returns a SMALL sage-green box "Object Y" BY VALUE directly back to Worker 1 (ink-black arrow, NOT through an object store). Worker 1 stores Y in a small pale blue-grey "In-process Store" box inside Node 1. One ochre-red caption: "small Y returns by value into the in-process store".
stage5: CONTENT: A message-flow diagram with THREE dashed muted-teal rounded Node containers in a row (dashed borders only, no black outer box): "Node 1" (left), "Node 2" (middle), "Node 3" (right). Node 1 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler" and a steel-blue "Worker 1"; inside Worker 1 a small white "Ownership Table" with columns [Obj | Task | Loc] and two rows: X and Y, and a small pale blue-grey "In-process Store". Node 2 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker 2", and a muted-teal "Object Store". Node 3 contains a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker 3", and a muted-teal "Object Store". THIS STAGE — Garbage collection: Worker 1's Ownership Table drops the X row (show the X row crossed out / faded). Both copies of "Object X" (the pinned one in Node 2's Object Store and the cached one in Node 3's Object Store) are shown faded/greyed with a small trash icon, meaning they are being deleted. IMPORTANT: the small sage-green "Object Y" stays SOLID inside Worker 1's In-process Store in Node 1 (still referenced), and the Y row in the Ownership Table stays solid. One ochre-red caption: "no references left -> every copy of X is safely deleted".
